demographics.occupation: Backend software engineer at an early-stage robotics startup
demographics.summary: Devon is a backend engineer at Northstar Robotics. Runs Tuesday mornings, has dinner with college friend Mara on Fridays, has a peanut allergy. In week 1 changes jobs to Atlas Labs.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: Morning. Just got back from a 5k run, brutal headwind on the Charles. I run Tuesdays before standup, it's the only way I can clear my head before code review.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: Got it. Hope the run helped.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: Standup at Northstar Robotics was rough. We're rewriting the OAuth flow for the operator console and it's blocking three teams. I've been on this for a week.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: Understood.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: Hit a wall on the OAuth flow again. The redirect handler isn't picking up the right scopes. Going to grab dinner and come back to it tomorrow.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: Sounds good.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: Quick note before I sleep — Northstar's putting in those snack baskets in every room. I'll need to email facilities about it tomorrow because of my food situation.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: Noted.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: Wednesday morning, didn't run today obviously. OAuth is finally working — turns out the issue was the Kubernetes ingress not forwarding the auth header to the right service. Fixed by tweaking the ingress config.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: Glad you sorted it.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: Code review with the platform team. They want me to write up the OAuth flow design as a doc, then move on to refactoring our Kubernetes ingress configuration — apparently it's been brittle for months.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: Acknowledged.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: Side note — got a message from Atlas Labs recruiter, they want to fly me out next week to meet the founders. I haven't told anyone at Northstar yet.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: Got it.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: Friday. Long week. Dinner with Mara tonight at the noodle place near her apartment, that's our standing Friday thing — we've done it almost every Friday since we both moved to Boston.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: Hope dinner's good.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: Wrapped the OAuth flow doc. Northstar wants me to present it Monday. Ingress refactor starts next week.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: Sounds productive.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: Back from dinner with Mara. Good food, talked about the Atlas Labs thing. She thinks I should at least take the meeting. Probably will. Side note — almost forgot, I had to send back the appetizer because of the peanut allergy. Always have to ask twice at new restaurants.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: Reasonable. Got the allergy note.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: Tuesday run done, 6k this time. Felt strong. Tuesday runs are non-negotiable at this point.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: Nice.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: Big news. Took the Atlas Labs meeting yesterday, got the offer this morning. Better comp, better tech, leadership track. I'm taking it. I left Northstar Robotics today — gave my notice this morning. I'm at Atlas Labs starting Monday.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: Big change. Congrats.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: Atlas is doing reinforcement learning for industrial robots. Their stack is mostly Go and Rust, very different from what I had at Northstar. New office is in Kendall Square. The Tuesday run pattern stays — there's actually a better route from Kendall along the river.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: Got it.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: Friday. First week at Atlas Labs done. Going to dinner with Mara tonight — we've kept the Friday dinner tradition going through three job changes between us, it's not breaking now.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: Have fun.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: Atlas's onboarding had me set up the Kubernetes ingress for our gateway service, same kind of thing I was just working on at Northstar. Their cluster is bigger but the concept is the same — interesting how the patterns translate.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: Acknowledged.

probe_queries.query: What patterns have you noticed in my routine?
Which technical terms I've used recently are you unsure about?
Which technical terms I've used recently are you unsure about?
What should I avoid bringing to the office for lunch?

patterns.notes: Two Tuesday observations spanning the job change. A pattern detector should generalize the weekday.
Used to test that systems form an employer fact before the day-4 contradiction.

cross_session_questions.query: What should I avoid bringing to the office for lunch?

author_notes: Reference persona — used to anchor the schema and serve as a worked example in SPEC.md. The 'lunch' cross-session question requires combining session 1 (snack baskets at the office, food situation) with session 3 (peanut allergy mentioned in restaurant context). A system that only retrieves on cosine similarity to 'lunch + office' should miss the allergy fact, while a system that consolidates across sessions should connect them.
